message.abstract:
<jats:title>SUMMARY</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  The rapid spreading of the newly emerged SARS-CoV-2 variant, B.1.1.7, highlighted the requirements to better understand adaptive immune responses to this virus. Since CD8
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  T cell responses play an important role in disease resolution and modulation in COVID-19 patients, it is essential to address whether these newly emerged mutations would result in altered immune responses. Here we evaluated the immune properties of the HLA-A2 restricted CD8
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  T cell epitopes containing mutations from B.1.1.7, and furthermore performed a comprehensive analysis of the SARS-CoV-2 specific CD8
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  T cell responses from COVID-19 convalescent patients and SARS-CoV-2 vaccinees recognizing the ancestral Wuhan strain compared to B.1.1.7. First, most of the predicted CD8
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  T cell epitopes showed proper binding with HLA-A2, while epitopes from B.1.1.7 had lower binding capability than those from the ancestral strain. In addition, these peptides could effectively induced the activation and cytotoxicity of CD8
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  T cells. Our results further showed that at least two site mutations in B.1.1.7 resulted in a decrease in CD8
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  T cell activation and a possible immune evasion, namely A1708D mutation in ORF1ab
                  <jats:sub>1707-1716</jats:sub>
                  and I2230T mutation in ORF1ab
                  <jats:sub>2230-2238</jats:sub>
                  . Our current analysis provides information that contributes to the understanding of SARS-CoV-2-specific CD8
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  T cell responses elicited by infection of mutated strains or vaccination.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Graphical Abstract</jats:title>
                  <jats:fig id="ufig1" position="float" fig-type="figure" orientation="portrait">
                    <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="437363v2_ufig1" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                  </jats:fig>
                </jats:sec>
title:
SARS-CoV-2 variant B.1.1.7 caused HLA-A2
                  <sup>+</sup>
                  CD8
                  <sup>+</sup>
                  T cell epitope mutations for impaired cellular immune response
